question: what is the capital of france
label: general
answer: The capital of France is Paris. 🗼 Paris is also the largest city in France with a population of about 2.1 million. It is famous for the Eiffel Tower the Louvre Museum and being a global centre of art culture and fashion.

question: what is the capital of england
label: general
answer: The capital of England (and the United Kingdom) is London. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 London is one of the world's most influential cities home to the British Parliament Buckingham Palace the River Thames and over 9 million people.

question: who invented the telephone
label: general
answer: The telephone was invented by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876. 📞 He made the first successful phone call saying: "Mr. Watson come here. I want to see you." Bell's invention revolutionised global communication.

question: who invented the light bulb
label: general
answer: Thomas Edison is credited with developing the practical incandescent light bulb in 1879. 💡 He also invented the phonograph and motion picture camera. Edison held over 1000 patents and is one of the greatest inventors in history.

question: what is the solar system
label: general
answer: The Solar System consists of the Sun and everything gravitationally bound to it. ☀️ It has 8 planets: Mercury Venus Earth Mars Jupiter Saturn Uranus Neptune. Also includes moons asteroids comets and dwarf planets like Pluto.

question: which planet is largest
label: general
answer: Jupiter is the largest planet in our Solar System. 🪐 It is so large that all other planets could fit inside it! Jupiter is a gas giant with a famous Great Red Spot — a storm that has lasted over 400 years. It has at least 95 known moons.

question: what is the speed of light
label: general
answer: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299 792 458 metres per second (about 300 000 km/s). ⚡ Light takes about 8 minutes to travel from the Sun to Earth. Einstein's theory states nothing with mass can travel faster than light.

question: who wrote romeo and juliet
label: general
answer: Romeo and Juliet was written by William Shakespeare around 1594-1596. 📖 It is a tragedy about two young lovers from rival families (Montague and Capulet) in Verona Italy. It is one of the most performed plays in the world.

question: what is the united nations
label: general
answer: The United Nations (UN) is an international organisation founded in 1945 after World War 2. 🌍 It has 193 member states. Its goals include maintaining peace protecting human rights and promoting development. Headquarters: New York City.

question: what is the human rights
label: general
answer: Human rights are fundamental rights and freedoms that every person is entitled to regardless of nationality sex ethnicity religion or any other status. 🌍 They include: right to life freedom of speech right to education freedom from torture and right to equality.

question: how many continents are there
label: general
answer: There are 7 continents: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Australia/Oceania and Antarctica. 🌍 Some countries teach 6 continents by combining Europe and Asia as Eurasia. Africa has the most countries (54); Antarctica has none.

question: what is water made of
label: general
answer: Water is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom giving it the chemical formula H₂O. 💧 It covers about 71% of Earth's surface. Water is essential for all known life. It can exist as solid (ice) liquid (water) or gas (steam/water vapour).

question: what is the tallest mountain
label: general
answer: Mount Everest is the tallest mountain above sea level standing at 8 849 metres (29 032 feet) above sea level. 🏔️ It is located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and Tibet. Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay first summited it in 1953.
